---
title: "Exploring the Gaussian privacy tradeoff"
description: "A short walkthrough of the privacy–utility curve for a Gaussian noise mechanism, with an interactive plot you can load on demand."
date: 2026-06-15
categories: [differential privacy, hypothesis testing, interactives]
---

Differential privacy often enters the story through a **hypothesis-testing** lens: how much can an
adversary distinguish neighboring datasets after we add noise? For a Gaussian mechanism with fixed
sensitivity, the feasible region in ROC space depends on the noise scale.

The lecture on [DP as hypothesis testing](../../../content/lectures/03-hypothesis_testing/learn.ipynb)
develops this picture in full. Below is the same interactive widget used in that lecture — it
loads automatically after the page is ready (the first load downloads a Python runtime in the browser).

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| message: false
#| warning: false
from scipy.stats import norm

from libdpy.visualization.privacy_plots import PrivacyPlot

PrivacyPlot(
    sensitivity=1,
    std=1.5,
    distribution_types=[norm],
).embed()
```

Try increasing the noise scale and watch how the tradeoff curve moves. The static site ships this
plot as a self-contained WASM app so it keeps working on GitHub Pages without a live server.
